concept: **黄金矿工 (Gold Miner)** Players control a stationary miner at the top of the screen, whose claw automatically swings left and right across a 2D mine shaft. A single tap anywhere on the screen deploys the claw downwards, which then retracts, pulling up the first item it hooks. Different items like small gold nuggets ($50), large gold nuggets ($500), rocks ($10), and diamonds ($600) have varying monetary values and weights, with heavier items significantly slowing the claw's retraction speed by up to 50%. Each level presents a specific money target, such as $500, which players must achieve within a strict time limit, typically 60 seconds, displayed on the UI. Successfully meeting the target advances the player to the next level, where money targets increase by approximately $100 and item layouts become more complex, while failure results in a game over. After each level, players can access a shop to spend earned money on single-use power-ups like dynamite to destroy unwanted grabbed items or strength potions to temporarily increase claw retraction speed by 50%.
name: %E9%Bb%84%E9%87%91%E7%9F%Bf%E5%B7%A5 %E5%8F%Af%E5%Ad%98%E6%A1%A3 %E5%A4%8D%E6%B4%Bb %E5%8F%8C%E4%Ba%Ba%E6%A8%A1%E5%Bc%8F %E8%81%94%E6%9C%Ba%E5%Af%B9%E6%88%98

concept: **Leps World Jump N Run Games** Players control Lep the leprechaun in a 2D side-scrolling platformer, navigating through 160 levels across 6 distinct world themes. Movement is controlled via a virtual D-pad on the left side of the screen, while a dedicated jump button on the right allows players to tap for a standard jump or hold for a higher leap. Lep's objective is to reach the flag at the end of each level, collecting scattered gold coins for score and cloverleaves to restore one of his three health points, while avoiding or defeating 9 unique enemy types by jumping on their heads. Levels progressively introduce more complex platforming challenges, increased enemy density, and environmental hazards like bottomless pits, with losing all health or falling into a pit resulting in a level restart. Successfully completing levels unlocks subsequent stages, and players can aim to collect all gold coins within a level to achieve a perfect score or unlock bonus content.
name: Leps World Jump N Run Games

concept: **Traffic Run** Players control a vehicle that automatically stops at the edge of multi-lane intersections, with perpendicular traffic continuously flowing across the screen. The core mechanic involves a single tap anywhere on the screen to accelerate the player's vehicle forward, requiring precise timing to navigate through gaps in the oncoming traffic without collision. Each level consists of a series of such intersections, with successful crossings awarding points and occasionally spawning collectible coins. Difficulty scales by progressively increasing the density of cross-traffic (e.g., from one car every 3 seconds to one every 1.5 seconds), the speed of opposing vehicles, and the number of lanes at each intersection. Players can spend collected coins to unlock new vehicle skins, which offer visual variety but do not alter gameplay mechanics, and the run immediately ends upon any collision with another vehicle.
name: Traffic Run

concept: **Worms Zone Io Hungry Snake** Players control a worm that continuously moves forward within a large, circular 2D arena, steering its direction by dragging a finger across the screen. The primary objective is to grow the worm's length and mass by consuming scattered colored food pellets, each granting approximately 5 mass points, and the remains of eliminated opponents. A tactical speed boost can be activated by double-tapping or holding the screen, consuming 10 mass points per second, allowing for aggressive maneuvers to encircle and eliminate other player-controlled worms whose heads collide with the player's body. Various temporary power-ups, such as a food magnet or a short invincibility shield, spawn periodically and can be collected to gain strategic advantages for 5-10 seconds. The game ends for a player if their worm's head collides with another worm's body or the arena boundary, with the ultimate goal being to achieve the highest mass and climb the real-time leaderboard.
name: Worms Zone Io Hungry Snake

concept: **Jelly Shift Obstacle Course** Players control a continuously forward-moving jelly character in a 3D obstacle course, with the core mechanic involving swiping up or down on the screen to instantly shift the jelly's vertical height between three distinct states (e.g., tall, medium, flat). The objective is to navigate through dynamically appearing obstacles like varying height barriers, low bars, and high runners by matching the jelly's shape to the gaps, with any collision resulting in immediate failure and a level restart. Successfully passing multiple obstacles without collision triggers "Jelly Fever," granting a temporary speed boost for 5 seconds and increasing diamond collection rates. Progression occurs across 200 unique levels spanning dozens of environments, which unlock sequentially, with difficulty scaling through increased obstacle frequency (e.g., from one every 2 seconds to one every 0.7 seconds) and more intricate shape-matching sequences. Collected diamonds can be spent in a character selection menu to unlock new jelly skins, offering visual customization.
name: Jelly Shift Obstacle Course

concept: **Bounce And Collect:** Players initiate a round by tapping the top of the screen to drop a single ball into a vertical, physics-based arena filled with pegs, gates, and multiplier zones. The ball bounces dynamically, interacting with the environment, aiming to pass through specific multiplier gates (e.g., x2, x5, +10 balls) to increase the total ball count or score before falling into collection bins at the bottom. Each level presents a unique static or dynamic layout of obstacles and multipliers, requiring strategic initial drop placement (e.g., by swiping left/right to position the drop point) to optimize ball multiplication and score accumulation. Progression involves completing a series of increasingly complex levels, each with a target score or a limited number of initial balls (e.g., 5 drops per level), unlocking new ball skins or starting bonuses (e.g., start with 2 balls) upon reaching specific cumulative score milestones. Difficulty scales through more intricate level designs featuring moving obstacles, smaller multiplier targets, and higher score requirements, with the primary goal being to achieve the highest possible score within the given constraints of each level.
name: Bounce And Collect

concept: **Coffee Stack:** Players control a stack of coffee cups that automatically moves forward along a linear path, using horizontal swipes (left/right) to navigate between 3-5 lanes. The core mechanic involves collecting empty cups by running over them, adding to the stack, and then passing through various gates that apply modifications like filling with coffee, adding sleeves, or attaching lids, each step increasing the cup's value. Obstacles, such as barriers or grinders, reduce the stack size by a fixed number of cups (e.g., -1 cup per collision), requiring skillful lane changes to maintain a large stack. At the end of each level, players serve the accumulated, customized coffee cups to a line of customers, earning coins based on the number and quality of cups delivered (e.g., a fully customized cup earns 10 coins, an empty cup earns 1 coin). Coins collected are used in a meta-game to unlock new coffee recipes, upgrade shop aesthetics, or purchase permanent bonuses like starting with 2 cups or increasing base cup value by 10%, with difficulty scaling through longer paths, more complex gate sequences, and increased obstacle density.
name: Coffee Stack
developer: Rollic Games Oyun Yazilim ve Pazarlama Anonim Sirketi

concept: **Bridge Race:** Players control a character in a competitive race against 3 AI opponents, automatically moving forward and using horizontal swipes (left/right) to steer across a multi-lane track. The core mechanic involves collecting colored blocks scattered across the track, which are automatically added to the player's inventory, and then using these blocks to construct bridges over designated water gaps by simply running onto the bridge-building zones. Players can physically collide with opponents to knock them over, causing them to drop a portion of their collected blocks (e.g., 5 blocks), which can then be picked up by any player. Progression occurs across over 1000 levels, each featuring increasingly complex race tracks with multiple bridge segments and strategic block placement, with new character skins, block designs, and color palettes unlocking upon completing specific level milestones or achieving top-3 finishes. Difficulty scales through longer races, more frequent and wider water gaps requiring more blocks, and more aggressive AI opponents who actively target the player or block collection paths, with the primary objective being to be the first to cross the finish line.
name: Bridge Race

concept: **Snake Vs Block:** Players control a snake composed of multiple balls that automatically moves forward on a vertical, endless track, using horizontal swipes (left/right) to steer the snake across the screen. The core mechanic involves guiding the snake to collide with numbered blocks, where each collision reduces the snake's current length by the block's displayed number (e.g., a '10' block reduces length by 10 balls). Simultaneously, players must collect individual balls scattered on the track, which instantly increase the snake's length by 1 ball, aiming to maintain the longest possible snake. The game features an endless progression where blocks spawn continuously at increasing speeds and with higher numerical values (e.g., blocks ranging from 1 to 50 initially, scaling up to 100+), while collectible balls also appear at varying frequencies. The run ends immediately when the snake's length reaches zero, with the primary objective being to achieve the highest possible score, calculated by the total distance traveled and the maximum snake length achieved during a single run.
name: Snake Vs Block

concept: **Traffic Run:** Players control a single vehicle positioned at a stop line before an intersection, using a tap-and-hold mechanic to accelerate forward and releasing the tap to brake and stop. The core objective is to safely cross multi-lane intersections where continuous streams of AI-controlled traffic flow perpendicularly, requiring precise timing to navigate through gaps in the traffic. Each level presents a distinct intersection layout, starting with 2-lane roads and progressing to 4-lane or more complex junctions with varying traffic speeds (e.g., AI cars travel at 10-20 units/second) and densities. Successfully crossing an intersection completes the current level and awards coins (e.g., 10 coins per level), which can be used to unlock new vehicle skins or visual upgrades that do not affect gameplay mechanics. The game features an endless level progression, with difficulty scaling through increased traffic volume, faster AI vehicles, and more intricate intersection designs, and the run ends immediately upon any collision with another vehicle, requiring a restart of the current level.
name: Traffic Run

concept: **Cannon Shot:** Players complete physics-based puzzles by guiding a fixed number of balls from a cannon into designated buckets. To play, players drag and drop or rotate pre-placed environmental objects like ramps and deflectors on the screen using touch gestures, strategically altering the balls' trajectory. Once objects are positioned, players tap the cannon to fire a set quantity of balls, typically 10-20, which then fall under gravity and interact with the environment. A level is won when all 2-5 buckets are filled with at least one ball, and lost if the player runs out of balls before achieving this goal. Completing levels sequentially unlocks new, more complex puzzle layouts and occasionally new cannon skins, with difficulty scaling through increased object interaction complexity and stricter ball count limits.
name: Cannon Shot

concept: **Spill It:** Players complete physics-based puzzles by dropping a limited number of balls to knock over all glasses on the screen. To play, players tap anywhere on the screen to drop a single ball from a fixed top-center spawn point, with rapid taps allowing for quick successive drops at a rate of approximately 0.2 seconds per ball. Each level features a unique arrangement of 3-10 glasses on various platforms, requiring strategic timing and placement of the 5-10 available balls to achieve a chain reaction or direct impact. A level is won when all glasses are toppled, and lost if the player expends all balls without achieving this objective. Successfully completing levels unlocks the next sequential challenge and awards coins, which can be spent in a shop to unlock cosmetic ball skins and visual themes.
name: Spill It

concept: **Long Neck Run:** Players control a character in an endless runner, automatically moving forward on a single lane, using left/right swipes to navigate horizontally. The core mechanic revolves around the character's neck length, which starts at 5 segments; collecting rings matching the character's current color adds 1 segment, while hitting a wrong-colored ring removes 2 segments. Obstacles like low barriers or zip lines require a minimum neck height to pass, otherwise, the character loses 5 neck segments or the run ends if the neck is too short. Difficulty scales as the procedurally generated track increases in complexity and the character's speed incrementally rises by 5% every 15 seconds. The run concludes if the neck length reaches zero or the character falls off the track, with the primary goal being to achieve the highest distance and neck length to earn keys and gemstones for unlocking cosmetic skins.
name: Long Neck Run

concept: **Mob Control:** Players control a cannon to generate and grow a mob of units, aiming to destroy an enemy base in a lane-based combat system. To play, players tap and hold the screen to continuously fire units at a rate of 5 units per second, dragging horizontally to aim the cannon across a fixed firing arc. Units automatically advance down the lane, passing through various gates that either multiply their count (e.g., x2, +50) or reduce it (e.g., -10, /2), with the objective of maximizing mob size to overwhelm an opposing mob and destroy their base, which typically has 100 HP. Winning battles awards coins and booster packs containing cards used to unlock and upgrade new cannon types, unit types, and powerful Champion units, with upgrades increasing stats like cannon fire rate by 10% or unit HP by 5%. Difficulty scales by increasing enemy mob strength, base HP, and the complexity of gate layouts, while a loss occurs if the player's mob is eliminated or their own base is destroyed.
name: Mob Control

concept: **Square Bird Flappy Chicken:** Players control a square bird automatically moving horizontally, tapping the screen to lay eggs and build a temporary tower to clear obstacles. Tapping once lays a single egg, increasing the bird's height, while holding the tap continuously lays eggs at a rate of 0.1 seconds per egg, with releasing the tap causing the bird to descend. The objective is to precisely time egg-laying to navigate through horizontal barriers of varying heights without collision, as hitting any part of an obstacle or falling off-screen immediately ends the run. Difficulty scales as the bird's horizontal speed increases by 5% every 20 seconds, and obstacles become more frequent and complex. Achieving three consecutive "perfect landings" on the ground activates "Square Bird Fever" mode for 5 seconds, granting temporary invincibility and a score multiplier.
name: Square Bird Flappy Chicken

concept: **Go Escape Casual Ball Games:** Players control a spherical ball that automatically rolls forward on a series of interconnected platforms, navigating a 3D environment. The primary control involves tapping the screen to make the ball jump, with jump height and distance determined by the duration of the tap (short tap for small jump, longer tap for higher/further jump). Each level presents a unique, pre-designed sequence of platforms, including static, moving, and vanishing types, along with various obstacles like stationary blocks and dynamically moving barriers that require precise timing to avoid. Successfully guiding the ball from the starting platform to the designated end platform completes the level, unlocking the next stage in a linear progression of over 100 levels, each increasing in complexity with tighter jump windows and more intricate obstacle patterns. Collision with any obstacle or falling off a platform results in immediate failure, requiring a restart of the current level, with the goal being to achieve a perfect run through each stage. Difficulty scales through increasingly complex level layouts, faster moving obstacles, and more precarious platform arrangements.
name: Go Escape Casual Ball Games

concept: **Hide Ball Brain Teaser Games:** Players are presented with a static 2D puzzle screen containing one or more "good balls" and several "monster" spawn points, with the objective to protect the good balls. The core mechanic involves drawing or placing various geometric shapes (e.g., lines, squares, circles) onto the screen using a tap-and-drag interface, which then become solid, physics-enabled objects. After the player finishes placing shapes, a timer begins (e.g., 5 seconds), during which monsters activate and attempt to reach the good balls, rolling or moving across the screen. Winning a level requires successfully shielding all good balls from any contact with monsters until the timer expires, with failure occurring if any monster touches a good ball. Progression occurs through a series of 100+ levels, each introducing more complex layouts, increased numbers of good balls (up to 5) and monsters (up to 3), and a limited "ink" or "shape budget" for placing protective objects. Difficulty scales by reducing the available drawing area, increasing monster speed, and requiring more intricate structural designs to create effective, physics-stable shelters.
name: Hide Ball Brain Teaser Games

concept: **Red Wrecker:** Red Wrecker is a physics-based puzzle game where players must remove all red-colored geometric shapes from the screen while ensuring all green and blue shapes remain within the play area. Players interact with the puzzle by tapping on specific shapes, typically green or blue ones, to either remove them from the screen or activate a special property (e.g., a "bomb" shape that explodes, or a "pivot" shape that rotates). The game's physics engine simulates gravity and collisions, causing shapes to fall, slide, and interact dynamically after each player action, requiring careful planning of tap sequences and timing. A level is won when all red shapes have fallen completely off the bottom edge of the screen, and no green or blue shapes have exited the play area, progressing players through 100 distinct, increasingly complex levels. Difficulty scales by introducing more intricate shape arrangements, requiring precise timing for taps, incorporating new interactive shape types with unique physics properties, and sometimes limiting the number of taps allowed per level. Failure occurs if any green or blue shape falls off the screen, or if any red shape remains on screen after all possible interactive actions have been exhausted, prompting a level restart.
name: Red Wrecker
